meta_data.Data_Name: Paper-level data deposit from City-Region System Toolbox on Zenodo
meta_data.Data_summary: A public repository providing the study outputs as a global spatial dataset of delineated city-regions for multiple travel-time cutoffs plus accompanying country-level summary tools. It is the main distribution point for the paper's reusable city-region data product and supporting files used to reproduce population distributions by city-region type.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: The global spatial dataset of city regions for different travel time cutoffs is available on the City Region System Toolbox from Zenodo via https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.11187634 ref. 32, Location: Data availability, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Global Human Settlement Layer Population GHS-POP R2022A
meta_data.Data_summary: A gridded global population dataset from the GHSL program used to allocate population to grid cells and to compute population totals and distributions for urban centers and delineated catchment areas and city-regions in the main results.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: we then combine it with the Global Human Settlement Layer Population GHS POP to allocate population to individual pixels Methods, Location: Defining city regions, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Global Human Settlement Layer GHSL Data Package 2022
meta_data.Data_summary: A global settlement and built-up related data package from GHSL used as the basis to derive locations and populations of urban centers which are then classified into tiers and used as destinations for travel-time catchments.
meta_data.Category: Building footprints and land use maps
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: the locations and populations have been derived starting from the Global Human Settlement Layer, Location: Methods Identifying catchment areas Urban centers, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Global travel time to cities map cost surface and travel time grid
meta_data.Data_summary: A global gridded travel-time accessibility product built from a travel cost surface that encodes speeds over roads railroads navigable rivers walking surfaces terrain land cover elevation slope and border delays. It is used to compute least-cost travel times from each grid cell to the nearest urban center in each activity tier and to delineate minimum-travel-time catchment areas worldwide at about 1 km resolution.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: We use the most up to date global estimates of travel times available at a spatial resolution of about 1 km and build on earlier work, Location: Methods Travel time, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Suite of global accessibility indicators dataset
meta_data.Data_summary: A published global accessibility dataset family providing indicators and supporting layers related to travel time and access, referenced as earlier work the study builds on for global accessibility estimation across settlement sizes and for the travel time surfaces used in catchment delineation.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: We use the most up to date global estimates of travel times available at a spatial resolution of about 1 km and build on earlier work that applied the method across a spectrum of settlement sizes, Location: Methods Travel time, Confidence: medium

meta_data.Data_Name: Global maps of travel time to healthcare facilities
meta_data.Data_summary: A global gridded travel time dataset to healthcare facilities cited as an established method for estimating travel time to destinations. It is used as methodological grounding for the travel-time computation approach employed to generate travel times to urban centers.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: This is achieved using an established method that estimates the time required to reach each grid cell of the world’s surface, Location: Defining city regions, Confidence: medium

meta_data.Data_Name: Gridded Population of the World GPW
meta_data.Data_summary: A global gridded population dataset used as an alternative population surface to compute city-region and catchment populations and to test sensitivity of results to population data choices when classifying and summarizing urban centers and city-regions.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: the population of each city region is calculated by using the GHS POP, Gridded Population of the World, LandScan and WorldPop population datasets, Location: Methods City regions, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_summary: A global gridded population distribution dataset used as an alternative population surface to compute populations for urban centers and delineated city-regions and to assess robustness of population distributions across city-region types.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: the population of each city region is calculated by using the GHS POP, Gridded Population of the World, LandScan and WorldPop population datasets, Location: Methods City regions, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_summary: A global gridded population dataset used as an alternative population surface for computing urban center and city-region populations and for reducing bias in classifying urban centers by combining information from multiple population datasets.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: four different gridded global population datasets are utilized in this study, Location: Methods Identifying catchment areas Urban centers, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Global Administrative Areas Database GADM version 4.1
meta_data.Data_summary: A global administrative boundary dataset used to assign each delineated city-region to a country and to split multi-country city-regions into country segments for country-level population statistics.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: the country where the city region is located is identified by using the Global Administrative Areas Database, Location: Methods City regions, Confidence: high
